title: FEMS hygiene manager job: consolidate + prune + flag + promote (folded WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job)
goal: Implement a scheduled FEMS hygiene job that runs consolidation, pruning, flagging, and procedural promotion via a cost-effective model (no LLM in the runtime path; LLM only as opt-in step for consolidation summarization). Folded from WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job. Ports the battle-tested Memory Manager pattern from repo governance into the product Execution / Job Runtime.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/memory/hygiene.rs (HygieneJobRunner + HygieneTask + HygieneReport + ProceduralPromotion types). Wires into the existing Execution / Job Runtime (consumed by trait so no new scheduler). Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/hygiene_manager_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-157 (bitemporal indexing - hygiene needs to invalidate items, not delete), MT-158 (outcome feedback - hygiene uses outcome history for promotion decisions), MT-159 (pinned core - hygiene must not prune pinned items).
implementation_notes: Per WP-1-FEMS-Hygiene-Manager-Job preserved_intent and refinement D.3 'hygiene manager job for procedural promotion / GC'. Surface: `pub struct HygieneJobRunner<'a> { fems: &'a dyn FemsAccessor, action_catalog: &'a dyn KernelActionSubmitter }` with `pub fn run_once(config: HygieneConfig) -> Result<HygieneReport, HygieneError>`. HygieneTask enum: `Consolidate { max_pairs: u32 }`, `PruneStale { older_than: Duration, min_score: f64 }`, `FlagContradictions`, `PromoteProcedural { min_use_count: u32, min_pass_rate: f64 }`. HygieneReport: per-task `{ items_visited, items_acted_on, errors, duration_ms }`. Consolidation: find near-duplicate pairs (using existing FEMS embedding distance) and emit MemoryConsolidationCandidate via KernelActionCatalogV1 for review-gated merge; DO NOT auto-merge. Prune: items older than threshold AND score below min_score get invalidated (set invalidated_at via MT-157 bitemporal axis, not deleted). MUST skip pinned items (MT-159). Flag contradictions: pairs of items with high cosine similarity but contradicting outcomes (one PASS-attributed, one FAIL-attributed) flagged for operator review. Promote procedural: items used >=min_use_count times with PASS rate >=min_pass_rate become candidates for ProceduralPromotion (operator review then surface promotion). NO LLM required in the runtime path; an optional consolidation summarizer is opt-in only and runs out-of-band. Tests: each HygieneTask runs independently; pruned items get invalidated_at set (NOT deleted); pinned items skipped; consolidation emits candidates without auto-merging; report aggregates per-task counts.
risk_if_missed: Without active hygiene FEMS LongTermMemory grows unbounded, stale items pollute MemoryPack injection, and contradictions accumulate.
validator_focus: Hygiene never auto-merges or auto-deletes; all mutations are review-gated via KernelActionCatalogV1. Pruning uses bitemporal invalidation, not destructive delete. Pinned items always skipped.

minimum_controls: Hygiene never auto-merges or auto-deletes; all mutations are operator-review-gated via KernelActionCatalogV1.
Pruning uses bitemporal invalidation (sets invalidated_at) instead of destructive delete so replay still works.
Pinned items always skipped; hygiene cannot collide with operator pin decisions.
